tekst: That jingle NEVER gets old! Welcome back to the pod, people — the World Cup is absolutely cooking right now and I am FIRED UP! I'm Liam!
And I'm Mitch. Trying to stay calm and British about all of this, which is getting increasingly difficult. Let's get into it.

Okay RECAP TIME — did you SEE the USA game?! Four-one against Paraguay! FOUR. ONE. Folarin Balogun with two goals, the opening ceremony had a ten-year-old Norwegian kid singing with Katy Perry, and then the USA just absolutely DEMOLISHED Paraguay on the same night! Best. Day. Ever.

Yes, yes, settle down, Liam. It was a good performance, genuinely. But the real story of the day was Germany going SEVEN-one against Curaçao — and before you get too excited, Curaçao had twenty-five of their twenty-six players born outside the country. Kai Havertz with two goals. That was simply carnage. Also, Brazil could only draw one-all with Morocco, which is properly embarrassing, and apparently half of Brazil doesn't even care about this World Cup anymore. Wild times.

And Qatar drew Switzerland at the last minute! I read it was being compared to some Norwegian legend called Kjetil Rekdal scoring against Brazil? I don't know who that is but the internet was going absolutely mental.

Classic Liam, vaguely aware of football history but unable to place it in any context whatsoever. Right — let's talk about Piggy's competition. Simon is sitting top of the table on six points, which is frankly annoying for everyone else. Andrè, Marius, Jonas and Bjørn are all bunched up on five points — it's an absolute logjam. Then Ringstad and Tobias on four, and Håkon also on four propping things up.

Håkon sitting at the bottom is genuinely devastating. Did he just pick every draw as a win or something? Meanwhile Simon is out there being all smug on six points. I bet Simon watched a Germany seven-one and just went 'yes, exactly as I predicted.'

Australia beat Turkey two-nil which was quietly a solid result, and Scotland beat Haiti one-nil to lead Group C. Good for them, I suppose. Netherlands and Japan drew two-all which felt like a bit of a waste of a good game. Ringstad, if you had the Netherlands winning that one comfortably, that draw just absolutely did you in, mate.

Alright, coming up — Ivory Coast versus Ecuador, Sweden versus Tunisia, Spain versus Cape Verde, Belgium versus Egypt, and Saudi Arabia versus Uruguay! That Sweden game is interesting to me because like, Scandinavian pride, right? The Norwegian boys in the competition must have complicated feelings about cheering for Sweden.

I suspect they'll manage. Spain versus Cape Verde should be comfortable — Spain are top of Group H and look sharp. Belgium lead Group G and face Egypt, so Tobias, if you've gone against Belgium here, I genuinely worry for you. Also there's massive Norway news lurking in the background — Solbakken is being shielded before their opener, and Haaland's fitness is apparently confirmed. The national coach was asked about doubts and basically said if Erling's shot had hit the keeper, the keeper would be dead. Which is a sentence I didn't expect to read today.

That is genuinely the most terrifying scouting report I've ever heard. I love it. Marius and Jonas — if either of you have Norway doing anything interesting in your brackets, now is your moment to start believing. And Bjørn, you're right there on five points, one good round of results and you could be looking Simon right in the eye.

Simon will be sleeping well tonight, but this competition is wide open and it's only going to get messier. Andrè and Marius are breathing right down his neck, and honestly any of the top five could be leading by this time next episode.
This World Cup is insane and we are ONLY getting started. Thank you for listening, everyone — keep your predictions sharp, keep supporting your guys, and Håkon — mate — we believe in you. Sort of.
We'll be back with more chaos, more roasting, and more Liam confusing the offside rule. Until then — hit the outro!
